mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately depress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of observations where UNRATE crosses above 4.0.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real sales of final goods and housing starts signals broad-based demand contraction that reduces near-term corporate revenue forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply chain disruptions that force earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during inflationary regimes.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions produce positive or flat 5-day returns during CPIAUCSL > 200 periods, or Rank IC < 0.01 in high-inflation subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 200, persistent inflation erodes real discount rates and triggers central bank tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 200.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real sales of final goods signals data revision risk and weak underlying demand, causing investors to price in earnings downgrades and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS shows no predictive power for 20-day returns (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during periods of low unemployment reflects robust capacity utilization and pricing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns when UNRATE < 4.0, or out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01.
